title: Design a Rate Limiter
statementMd:
Design a sliding-window rate limiter constructed with `limit` and `window`.

- `allow(timestamp)` — return `true` if the request is permitted, else `false`. A request at time `t` is allowed when the number of previously-allowed requests with timestamp greater than `t - window` is strictly less than `limit`. Timestamps are non-decreasing. Only allowed requests are recorded.
hints: Keep the timestamps of the requests you have accepted.
A request at time t is allowed if fewer than `limit` accepted requests fall in the window (t - window, t].
Only record a timestamp when the request is actually allowed.
